_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the L3 'Full Leather Scout' keeper (NAFNet-deblurred) and apply the L3→L4 delta to produce the Lvl 4 'Basic Scale Mail' variant (Tier 2 base — first metal arrives). Experimental alternative to the tier-break archer_edit_L1_to_L4.json that anchors from L1_Base only.
_meta.input_image_content: UnitProgression/Archer/out/v3/L3/composite/archer_L3_v<keeper>_composited.png (v3 chain, user picks keeper at runtime)
_meta.input_strategy: Within-tier chain (single-input chained, locked 2026-05-07) — previous keeper composite is the sole input. No L1_Base scale anchor. Prompt focuses ONLY on the L3→L4 delta. Compare with archer_edit_L1_to_L4.json which anchors fresh from L1_Base.
history: v1 (2026-05-05): drafted as a chained alternative to the tier-break archer_edit_L1_to_L4.json. Modeled after archer_edit_L4_to_L5.json structure. Five adds: scale-mail sleeves, scale-mail hem band, leather forehead band on hood, belt re-strapped over scale-mail hem, brass grip-end binding rings on bow.

v2 (2026-05-05): SHORT-SLEEVE rework. Add (1) changed from full shoulder-to-wrist scale-mail sleeves to SHORT-SLEEVE scale mail covering only the deltoid + upper bicep down to mid-bicep, with bare skin from mid-bicep down to the leather wrist bracer. Reason: full sleeves under the leather bracer made the forearm scale-mail layer invisible at thumbnail scale; short sleeves give the new metal layer a clear silhouette read against bare skin. Negatives updated to forbid extending scale mail past mid-bicep.

v_no_stubble (2026-05-05): Stripped 'faint stubble shadow' from face descriptions. Reason: model was over-rendering the canonical faint stubble as a five o'clock shadow, especially at L8 where the half-visor frames the lower face. Stubble removed from positive prompt language; canonical face is now clean-shaven smooth peachy-tan skin with no facial hair. (Prompt body was reflowed to remove redundant separators after stubble removal.)

v3_detail_economy (2026-05-07): AD FeedbackV0 rewrite per CLAUDE.md Part 3 §23. (a) Replaced add (3) LEATHER FOREHEAD BAND with STEEL SKULLCAP UNDER HOOD — first metal headpiece, visible as polished steel brow rim inside the hood opening with two small iron rivets at the temples; pulls the metal-head schedule earlier (was L5 in old plan). (b) EDIT GOAL prologue: 'two hip pouches, knife sheath behind the right-hip pouch' → 'dagger sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip (no pouches)'. (c) Add (4) belt re-strapped: 'X-strap, both hip pouches, and the knife sheath' → 'X-strap and dagger sheath' (no pouches). (d) PRESERVE block: removed right-hip pouch + left-hip pouch + 'knife tucked behind the right-hip pouch' lines; dagger sheath now stands alone. (e) Negative block: removed 'DO NOT add a steel helmet, skullcap, or any metal head covering' (we ADD the skullcap); added blanket 'DO NOT add ANY hip pouch / belt pouch / leather satchel / hip-mounted accessory'. (f) Updated paths to v3 chain (input out/v3/L3/composite/, output out/v3/L4/variants/). Short-sleeve scale mail at L4-L6 retained; full-arm scale-mail (no bicep gap) arrives at L7 per AD.

v3_single_input (2026-05-07): Dropped L1_Base from image_urls per locked single-input chained rule (CLAUDE.md Part 1 §B revised). Stripped 'SECOND input image' / scale-anchor language from prompt body and FRAMING block heading. Sole input is the previous composited keeper. urls_before=2, urls_after=1, body_chars_removed=71.

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Archer/out/v3/L3/composite/archer_L3_v<keeper>_composited.png>> (CONTENT REFERENCE — the v3 L3 keeper to upgrade)
prompt: EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE additive upgrades to the archer in the input image (the 'Full Leather Scout' content reference, with both forearms leather-bracered, leather pauldron caps over each deltoid, X-strap chest harness, dagger sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip — NO hip pouches present, brown leather greaves knee-down, quilted red panels on the mustard-yellow tunic) to produce the 'Basic Scale Mail' variant — first metal arrives. KEEP EVERYTHING FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY AS-IS unless explicitly changed by the five upgrades below. ===== NEW ADDS FOR THIS EDIT (first metal arrives) ===== (1) SHORT-SLEEVE SCALE MAIL ON BOTH SHOULDERS — chunky overlapping cool silver-gray scale plates cover ONLY the deltoid and the UPPER BICEP on BOTH arms, ending at MID-BICEP in a clean horizontal scalloped edge (one or two scale-rows tall — short sleeves, NOT full sleeves). Each scale is a rounded shield-shape (semi-circular at the top, slightly tapered at the bottom), arranged in horizontal rows where each row overlaps the row below by about half a scale, painted in cool silver-gray steel with a soft top-down highlight on each scale and warm-brown shadow under the lower edge of each scale. The scales are CHUNKY and clearly individuated at the painted level (NOT fine chainmail rings, NOT small dots, NOT a flat texture). The leather pauldron caps from the input image sit ON TOP of the new short scale-mail sleeves at the shoulder — the pauldrons remain the outermost layer at the shoulder. CRITICAL — the LOWER BICEP, the ELBOW, and the UPPER FOREARM remain BARE warm peachy-tan skin (visible between the short scale-mail sleeve ending at mid-bicep and the leather wrist bracer ending at mid-forearm). The leather wrist bracers from the input image (bow arm with one cross-strap binding, draw arm with two cross-strap bindings) remain UNCHANGED — they continue to cover only the wrist up to mid-forearm exactly as in the input image, with bare skin above them up to the new short scale-mail sleeve. There is a clear bare-skin band of muscular bicep + elbow + upper forearm between the scale-mail short sleeve at mid-bicep and the leather bracer at mid-forearm. (2) SCALE-MAIL SHIRT VISIBLE AT TUNIC HEM (LIVERY PRESERVATION RULE) — CRITICAL: the mustard-yellow tunic with the quilted red center panel and quilted red side panels is the EMTD livery and MUST REMAIN THE DOMINANT CHEST GARMENT, FULLY VISIBLE FROM COLLAR TO HIP, NOT covered or replaced by scale mail. Add a band of cool silver-gray scale plates, three to four inches tall, peeking BELOW the lower edge of the tunic, sitting OVER the trousers and ABOVE the leather greaves. Reads as a scale-mail coat worn UNDER the tunic, with only this short hem band visible at the waist-to-upper-thigh transition. The scale mail does NOT extend UPWARD past the tunic hem; the tunic chest from collar to upper hip stays exactly as it was in the input image with the mustard yellow base, the quilted red center panel running collar-to-hem, and the quilted red side panels along the flanks. Same chunky overlapping-scale style as the new sleeves. (3) STEEL SKULLCAP UNDER THE HOOD (FIRST METAL HEADPIECE) — add a polished cool silver-gray steel skullcap on the head BENEATH the cloth hood. The crown of the cap is hidden under the hood, but its lower RIM is clearly visible as a clean curved steel band wrapping the forehead just inside the hood opening (sitting where the brow line meets the hood edge), about 1 inch tall, with TWO small painted iron rivets at the temples for functional read. The steel rim catches a soft top-down highlight reading as cool silver-gray, distinctly metal (NOT leather, NOT a leather forehead band). The hood color stays deep crimson red cloth, fully up; the steel skullcap rim is visible across the forehead inside the hood opening. This is the archer's FIRST METAL HEADPIECE — read it as the introduction of a metal helm tier. (4) BELT RE-STRAPPED OVER THE NEW SCALE-MAIL HEM — the wider single warm-brown leather belt from the input image is now visibly cinched OVER the new scale-mail-hem peek (the belt sits on top of the scale-mail layer rather than directly on the lower tunic edge), with the X-strap chest harness and the dagger sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip carried over and sitting cleanly over the new scale-mail layer. The brass rectangular buckle remains centered at the front; visible edge stitching on the belt remains. The belt remains CLEAN apart from the dagger sheath — no pouches, no satchels, no tool loops. (5) BOW UPGRADE — BRASS GRIP-END BINDING RINGS — add small polished brass bands at each END of the bow's central leather grip wrap (where the dark-brown leather grip meets the wood-brown limb, on BOTH the upper and lower side of the grip). Each band is a thin metallic brass ring wrapping the bow's central handle perpendicular to the limb axis, with a soft buttery-brass highlight catching the upper edge. Functional grip-transition reinforcement. The bow LIMB material (the curved wood part) remains warm wood-brown — only the brass binding rings at the grip ends are new. The leather grip wrap stays dark-brown. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== Every other element of the input image must remain identical: face (chiseled chibi jawline + closed-mouth half-smirk + asymmetric brow with anatomical-LEFT brow higher + warm brown eyes with single catchlight + small upturned nose + warm peachy-tan skin); deep crimson-red pointed cloth hood pulled fully up (now with the new STEEL SKULLCAP rim visible across the brow inside the hood opening, with two small iron rivets at the temples); matching crimson-red cape attached at both shoulders falling to knee length with soft folds and inward curl on viewer-LEFT; both forearms leather-bracered (bow arm single cross-strap, draw arm two cross-strap bindings — these bracers remain UNCHANGED, covering wrist to mid-forearm exactly as in the input image, with bare skin ABOVE the bracer up to the new short scale-mail sleeve at mid-bicep); both hands BARE peachy-tan; bare anatomical-LEFT hand gripping the recurve short bow vertically at hip height; the recurve short bow with warm wood-brown limbs + dark-brown leather grip wrap + pale cream bowstring (now also with the new brass grip-end binding rings); brown leather cylindrical back-quiver over anatomical-LEFT shoulder rising behind viewer-RIGHT side of head with three to four white-feather red-tip arrows; sleeveless mustard-yellow tunic with quilted red center panel and quilted red side panels (visibly dominant on the chest, unchanged); leather X-strap chest harness diagonally across front from anatomical-LEFT shoulder to anatomical-RIGHT hip with brass ring at crossing; dark-wood dagger in a leather sheath hanging from the belt at the anatomical-RIGHT hip (viewer-LEFT side of belt) with brass capstone on handle — STANDALONE, no pouches accompany it; leather pauldron caps over each deltoid (slightly darker brown than the bracers, single seam over each cap, NO studs — the pauldrons remain plain leather domes, now sitting OVER the new scale-mail-covered shoulders); brown leather greaves on lower legs from boot cuff to just below knee with single cross-strap binding each; olive forest-green knee-length trousers (visible above the greaves); chunky warm-brown leather boots with darker folded ankle cuff. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately 15-25 degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE BOW ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) hangs DOWN at his side with elbow only slightly bent, bow held VERTICALLY at HIP height in front of the hip. THE DRAW ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) bent at roughly 90 degrees, FOREARM HORIZONTAL ACROSS THE CHEST at upper-chest level, bracered hand at chest CENTER in TIGHT CLOSED FIST with KNUCKLES OUTWARD. Calm balanced HERO STANCE; NOT an active draw. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character at ~75% vertical fill (~765px tall), ~57% horizontal width (~580px wide). TOP of hood at ~13.5% from top of canvas. BOTTOM of boots at ~12% from bottom of canvas. Character roughly vertically centered, NOT bottom-anchored. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight, slight thickening in shadow recesses, slight thinning under the key light. Interior linework soft and value-driven, never hard black. Clean white background, no environmental context, no ground plane, no scenery, minimal contact shadow. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT cover the mustard-yellow tunic chest panel with scale mail; DO NOT replace the tunic with a scale-mail body coat; DO NOT extend the scale mail UPWARD past the tunic hem; DO NOT shrink the tunic into a thin strip or a short surcoat panel — the tunic must remain the DOMINANT chest garment, fully visible from collar to hip with the quilted red center panel and quilted red side panels and mustard-yellow base all clearly visible (the LIVERY PRESERVATION rule); DO NOT use FINE CHAINMAIL RING MESH or interlocking metal rings or fish-scale fine texture or scale that reads as small dots — the new scale-mail must use chunky overlapping rounded scales (rounded shield-shape, semi-circular top, half-scale row overlap); DO NOT extend the scale-mail sleeve past mid-bicep — the lower bicep, elbow, and upper forearm MUST remain bare peachy-tan skin (full-sleeve scale mail down to the wrist is WRONG for this level); DO NOT cover the entire arm between bracer and pauldron with scale mail; DO NOT extend the leather bracers further up the forearm to compensate — bracers stay wrist-to-mid-forearm exactly as in the input image; DO NOT render the scale-mail as a quilted diamond pattern or as fabric or as armor plates other than scale; DO NOT add brass studs to the leather pauldron caps — the pauldrons stay PLAIN leather (studs arrive at the next step); DO NOT replace the leather pauldron caps with metal pauldrons; DO NOT add a scale-mail coif at the neck (the coif arrives at the next step); DO NOT extend the scale-mail hem band into a longer skirt past mid-thigh (the longer skirt arrives at the next step); DO NOT add a crossbody arrow bandolier or any second crossbody strap — only the existing X-strap remains across the front of the chest; DO NOT add a leather over-jerkin or chest vest; DO NOT add gold trim, gold band, gold buckle, gold limb tips, or any GOLD accents — only BRASS at this step; DO NOT replace the steel skullcap with a leather forehead band — the head detail at this level is the steel skullcap rim, NOT a leather band; DO NOT add a tall conical helm, closed visor, or open-face plate helm — head is hooded with a steel skullcap rim only; DO NOT add steel breastplate, steel vambraces, or any plate armor; DO NOT add tassets or arming-skirt strips at the hip; DO NOT add a closed visor; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, or engraved patterns; DO NOT swap the hand holding the bow; DO NOT remove the X-strap; DO NOT remove the dagger sheath; DO NOT add ANY hip pouch, belt pouch, leather satchel, tool-loop ring, or hip-mounted accessory — the dagger sheath is the ONLY belt accessory at this level; DO NOT remove the leather greaves; DO NOT restore the white linen bandage on either arm — both forearms are leather-bracered (with cross-strap bindings) and the hands are BARE; DO NOT render either bracer as a plain leather sleeve without visible cross-strap bindings; DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT change any facial feature, hair, or skin tone; DO NOT change the hood color or shape; DO NOT change the cape color, length, or drape; DO NOT change the bow shape, position, or recurve profile; DO NOT change the arrow fletching colors (white feather + red tips); DO NOT add or remove arrows in the back-quiver; DO NOT change the back-quiver position; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background or add scenery; DO NOT drift the art style; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
